system.description:
Requirements:

  • At least one truly random (not planned or plot-essential) encounter remains in the current region. The character won’t know whether or not they meet this requirement until they attempt to resolve this activity. If no random encounters remain in the region, instead they will locate shortcuts that allow for travel at +1 mph for the current region.

  • The party is attempting stealth through the region (travel pace is either Slow or Crawl).

Time: The adventurer must spend the entire region undertaking this activity.

An adventurer can make a Stealth check to scout ahead slightly, guiding their companions to avoid the worst paths when sneaking through enemy territory. This could include regions such as Haunted Lands or Wartorn Kingdoms, or any nations or lands where they can reasonably expect the party to be swarmed by masses of foes if they are discovered.

Reminder for the rest of the party: Multitasking, such as attempting a journey activity while trying not to be discovered, may make it harder to attempt the check. This adds a +3 to the DC of all other Activities. The Sneak activity itself is unaffected by this, but everyone else’s Journey Activity is affected.

journeyProperties.criticalFailure: The character misreads the organization and movements of the enemy. Roll for an additional encounter, which happens immediately.
journeyProperties.failure: The adventurers experience random encounters as normal for the region.
journeyProperties.success: The character performing this activity can ask the Narrator to re-roll once during the current region when determining the next random encounter. Before revealing the encounter, the Narrator should give hints about what lies ahead, possibly using the Signs entry from the creatures’ description(s). Based on these hints, the character then chooses to stick with the current encounter or reroll. Note that planned or plot-essential encounters might supersede a particular “roll”, so it is at the Narrator’s discretion to allow the re-roll for a particular check.
journeyProperties.criticalSuccess: The party is able to avoid one random encounter in the current region.
